revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{intricate template}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> This template is used to configure the articles found in the '''''Selected quote''''' section found on most Wikipedia Portal main pages. Used it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected quote sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected quote/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="66.6%"> {{Selected quote | quote = <!--- Required ---> | quoted = <!--- Required ---> | omit_quoted = | comment = | dates = | source = | section = | archive = }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|comment=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''quote''' - is the quote verbiage, no quotation marks needed. * '''quoted''' - is name of the person who is being quoted, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''omit quoted''' - Remove the linked quoted person. Use this in the rare case there is not an article on the individual on WP, set it to '''''yes'''''. If this is the case, use the '''''|comment=''''' field for the quoted person's name. * '''comment''' - A brief, non-linked comment after the quoted individual's name. * '''dates''' - The author's date of birth and death, no parenthesis needed. * '''source''' - The work the quote is coming from, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''section''' - is the section in the work the quote appears. * '''archive''' - Using this field with a value of ''no'' will remove the '''More selected quotes''' link in the lower left corner.
== Example == The following example:
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE --> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Non-mainspace quotation templates]] }}</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} {{intricate template}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> This template is used to configure the articles found in the '''''Selected quote''''' section found on most Wikipedia Portal main pages. Used it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected quote sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected quote/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="66.6%"> {{Selected quote | quote = <!--- Required ---> | quoted = <!--- Required ---> | omit_quoted = | comment = | dates = | source = | section = | archive = }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|comment=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''quote''' - is the quote verbiage, no quotation marks needed. * '''quoted''' - is name of the person who is being quoted, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''omit quoted''' - Remove the linked quoted person. Use this in the rare case there is not an article on the individual on WP, set it to '''''yes'''''. If this is the case, use the '''''|comment=''''' field for the quoted person's name. * '''comment''' - A brief, non-linked comment after the quoted individual's name. * '''dates''' - The author's date of birth and death, no parenthesis needed. * '''source''' - The work the quote is coming from, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''section''' - is the section in the work the quote appears. * '''archive''' - Using this field with a value of ''no'' will remove the '''More selected quotes''' link in the lower left corner.
== Example == The following example:
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE --> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Non-mainspace quotation templates]] }}</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} {{intricate template}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> This template is used to configure the articles found in the '''''Selected quote''''' section found on most Wikipedia Portal main pages. Used it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected quote sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected quote/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="66.6%"> {{Selected quote | quote = <!--- Required ---> | quoted = <!--- Required ---> | omit_quoted = | comment = | dates = | source = | section = | archive = }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|comment=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''quote''' - is the quote verbiage, no quotation marks needed. * '''quoted''' - is name of the person who is being quoted, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''omit quoted''' - Remove the linked quoted person. Use this in the rare case there is not an article on the individual on WP, set it to '''''yes'''''. If this is the case, use the '''''|comment=''''' field for the quoted person's name. * '''comment''' - A brief, non-linked comment after the quoted individual's name. * '''dates''' - The author's date of birth and death, no parenthesis needed. * '''source''' - The work the quote is coming from, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''section''' - is the section in the work the quote appears. * '''archive''' - Using this field with a value of ''no'' will remove the '''More selected quotes''' link in the lower left corner.
== Example == The following example:
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE --> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Non-mainspace quotation templates]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] }}</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} {{intricate template}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> This template is used to configure the articles found in the '''''Selected quote''''' section found on most Wikipedia Portal main pages. Used it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected quote sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected quote/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="66.6%"> {{Selected quote | quote = <!--- Required ---> | quoted = <!--- Required ---> | omit_quoted = | comment = | dates = | source = | section = | archive = }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|comment=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''quote''' - is the quote verbiage, no quotation marks needed. * '''quoted''' - is name of the person who is being quoted, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''omit quoted''' - Remove the linked quoted person. Use this in the rare case there is not an article on the individual on WP, set it to '''''yes'''''. If this is the case, use the '''''|comment=''''' field for the quoted person's name. * '''comment''' - A brief, non-linked comment after the quoted individual's name. * '''dates''' - The author's date of birth and death, no parenthesis needed. * '''source''' - The work the quote is coming from, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''section''' - is the section in the work the quote appears. * '''archive''' - Using this field with a value of ''no'' will remove the '''More selected quotes''' link in the lower left corner.
== Example == The following example:
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE --> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Non-mainspace quotation templates]] [[Category:qq]] }}</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} {{intricate template}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> This template is used to configure the articles found in the '''''Selected quote''''' section found on most Wikipedia Portal main pages. Used it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected quote sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected quote/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="66.6%"> {{Selected quote | quote = <!--- Required ---> | quoted = <!--- Required ---> | omit_quoted = | comment = | dates = | source = | section = | archive = }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|comment=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''quote''' - is the quote verbiage, no quotation marks needed. * '''quoted''' - is name of the person who is being quoted, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''omit quoted''' - Remove the linked quoted person. Use this in the rare case there is not an article on the individual on WP, set it to '''''yes'''''. If this is the case, use the '''''|comment=''''' field for the quoted person's name. * '''comment''' - A brief, non-linked comment after the quoted individual's name. * '''dates''' - The author's date of birth and death, no parenthesis needed. * '''source''' - The work the quote is coming from, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''section''' - is the section in the work the quote appears. * '''archive''' - Using this field with a value of ''no'' will remove the '''More selected quotes''' link in the lower left corner.
== Example == The following example:
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE --> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Non-mainspace quotation templates]]
}}</includeonly>

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{intricate template}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> This template is used to configure the articles found in the '''''Selected quote''''' section found on most Wikipedia Portal main pages. Used it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected quote sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected quote/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="66.6%"> {{Selected quote | quote = <!--- Required ---> | quoted = <!--- Required ---> | omit_quoted = | comment = | dates = | source = | section = | archive = }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|comment=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''quote''' - is the quote verbiage, no quotation marks needed. * '''quoted''' - is name of the person who is being quoted, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''omit quoted''' - Remove the linked quoted person. Use this in the rare case there is not an article on the individual on WP, set it to '''''yes'''''. If this is the case, use the '''''|comment=''''' field for the quoted person's name. * '''comment''' - A brief, non-linked comment after the quoted individual's name. * '''dates''' - The author's date of birth and death, no parenthesis needed. * '''source''' - The work the quote is coming from, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''section''' - is the section in the work the quote appears. * '''archive''' - Using this field with a value of ''no'' will remove the '''More selected quotes''' link in the lower left corner.
== Example == The following example:
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE --> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Non-mainspace quotation templates]]
}}</includeonly> <noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{intricate template}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> This template is used to configure the articles found in the '''''Selected quote''''' section found on most Wikipedia Portal main pages. Used it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected quote sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected quote/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="66.6%"> {{Selected quote | quote = <!--- Required ---> | quoted = <!--- Required ---> | omit_quoted = | comment = | dates = | source = | section = | archive = }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|comment=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''quote''' - is the quote verbiage, no quotation marks needed. * '''quoted''' - is name of the person who is being quoted, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''omit quoted''' - Remove the linked quoted person. Use this in the rare case there is not an article on the individual on WP, set it to '''''yes'''''. If this is the case, use the '''''|comment=''''' field for the quoted person's name. * '''comment''' - A brief, non-linked comment after the quoted individual's name. * '''dates''' - The author's date of birth and death, no parenthesis needed. * '''source''' - The work the quote is coming from, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''section''' - is the section in the work the quote appears. * '''archive''' - Using this field with a value of ''no'' will remove the '''More selected quotes''' link in the lower left corner.
== Example == The following example:
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE --> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Non-mainspace quotation templates]]
}}</includeonly>

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{intricate template}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> This template is used to configure the articles found in the '''''Selected quote''''' section found on most Wikipedia Portal main pages. Used it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected quote sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected quote/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="66.6%"> {{Selected quote | quote = <!--- Required ---> | quoted = <!--- Required ---> | omit_quoted = | comment = | dates = | source = | section = | archive = }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|comment=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''quote''' - is the quote verbiage, no quotation marks needed. * '''quoted''' - is name of the person who is being quoted, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''omit quoted''' - Remove the linked quoted person. Use this in the rare case there is not an article on the individual on WP, set it to '''''yes'''''. If this is the case, use the '''''|comment=''''' field for the quoted person's name. * '''comment''' - A brief, non-linked comment after the quoted individual's name. * '''dates''' - The author's date of birth and death, no parenthesis needed. * '''source''' - The work the quote is coming from, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''section''' - is the section in the work the quote appears. * '''archive''' - Using this field with a value of ''no'' will remove the '''More selected quotes''' link in the lower left corner.
== Example == The following example:
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE --> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Non-mainspace quotation templates]]
}}</includeonly> [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23582763848|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Documentation subpage}} {{intricate template}} <!-- PLEASE ADD CATEGORIES AND INTERWIKIS AT THE BOTTOM OF THIS PAGE --> This template is used to configure the articles found in the '''''Selected quote''''' section found on most Wikipedia Portal main pages. Used it on the article pages that are linked to the Selected quote sub-page, e.g. '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected quote/1'''. For the quickest and easiest way to create the '''Portal:''Foo''/Selected ''bar''''' sub-page and related article links, use one of the {{tl|Numbered subpages}} templates.
== Template layout == <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext" width="66.6%"> {{Selected quote | quote = <!--- Required ---> | quoted = <!--- Required ---> | omit_quoted = | comment = | dates = | source = | section = | archive = }} </syntaxhighlight>
== Usage == It is unnecessary to include braces, quotation marks or other wiki-markup in the template. The '''''|comment=''''' field does allow the use of wiki-markup at the contributor's discretion.
* '''quote''' - is the quote verbiage, no quotation marks needed. * '''quoted''' - is name of the person who is being quoted, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''omit quoted''' - Remove the linked quoted person. Use this in the rare case there is not an article on the individual on WP, set it to '''''yes'''''. If this is the case, use the '''''|comment=''''' field for the quoted person's name. * '''comment''' - A brief, non-linked comment after the quoted individual's name. * '''dates''' - The author's date of birth and death, no parenthesis needed. * '''source''' - The work the quote is coming from, it will automatically Wikilink. * '''section''' - is the section in the work the quote appears. * '''archive''' - Using this field with a value of ''no'' will remove the '''More selected quotes''' link in the lower left corner.
== Example == The following example:
<syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }} </syntaxhighlight>
will produce:
{{Selected quote | quote = Good wine is a good familiar creature if it be well used. | quoted = William Shakespeare | dates = 1564-1616 | comment = Dead English guy | source = Othello | section = II. iii. (315) }}
== See also == {{Portal template list}}
<includeonly>{{Sandbox other|| <!-- ADD CATEGORIES BELOW THIS LINE -->
}}</includeonly> <noinclude> [[Category:Wikipedia Portal templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Non-mainspace quotation templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24975162458|time triggered pipeline]]
